channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former Massachusetts Congressman Barney Frank dies at 86
item.description: Frank was a liberal icon and gay-rights pioneer who represented Massachusetts in Congress for more than three decades. He had been receiving hospice care for congestive heart failure.
item.content:encoded: Frank was a liberal icon and gay-rights pioneer who represented Massachusetts in Congress for more than three decades. He had been receiving hospice care for congestive heart failure.
item.dc:creator: Adam Reilly

item.title: How Tuesday's primary elections played out in 6 states
item.description: Six states voted in primary elections Tuesday, with Republicans in Kentucky and Georgia voting on candidates that could reshape Congress in November.
item.content:encoded: Six states voted in primary elections Tuesday, with Republicans in Kentucky and Georgia voting on candidates that could reshape Congress in November.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Trump-backed Ed Gallrein defeats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky GOP primary
item.description: President Trump once again proves his political strength in Republican primaries, helping to oust Congressman Thomas Massie, a frequent critic, in Tuesday night's elections.
item.content:encoded: President Trump once again proves his political strength in Republican primaries, helping to oust Congressman Thomas Massie, a frequent critic, in Tuesday night's elections.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche defends new $1.8B Anti-Weaponization Fund
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche is defending a new $1.8 billion Department of Justice fund for people who claim they were targets of politicized prosecutions.
item.content:encoded: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche is defending a new $1.8 billion Department of Justice fund for people who claim they were targets of politicized prosecutions.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas

item.title: Denver has a plan to heat and cool buildings without fossil fuels. It involves … sewage?
item.description: Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.
item.content:encoded:
Like many cities, Denver's largest source of greenhouse gas emissions is its buildings. Heating and cooling skyscrapers requires a lot of fossil fuels. Now, the city is trying a surprising solution.

(Image credit: Aaron Ontiveroz)
item.dc:creator: Ishan Thakore

item.title: Lawmakers grill FAA's Bryan Bedford on safety and air traffic controller shortage
item.description: FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford faces tough questions from lawmakers about the state of the nation's air traffic control system and its efforts to increase staffing.
item.content:encoded: FAA Administrator Bryan Bedford faces tough questions from lawmakers about the state of the nation's air traffic control system and its efforts to increase staffing.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: As states enact changes to SNAP benefits, recipients face new challenges
item.description: States are phasing in major changes to food assistance. We hear from people who are feeling the impact and from Ty Jones Cox of the Center on Budget and Policy Priorities.
item.content:encoded: States are phasing in major changes to food assistance. We hear from people who are feeling the impact and from Ty Jones Cox of the Center on Budget and Policy Priorities.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: A look at primary results from around the country, Trump and Vance share conflicting messages on state of Iran war, shooting at San Diego mosque being investigated as possible "hate crime."
item.content:encoded: A look at primary results from around the country, Trump and Vance share conflicting messages on state of Iran war, shooting at San Diego mosque being investigated as possible "hate crime."
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Russian President Putin meets with China's Xi Jinping, days after Trump's visit
item.description: President Vladimir Putin is in Beijing for talks with President Xi Jinping, in a closely watched visit that comes just days after President Trump's high-profile trip to China.
item.content:encoded: President Vladimir Putin is in Beijing for talks with President Xi Jinping, in a closely watched visit that comes just days after President Trump's high-profile trip to China.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Pak

item.title: 'Wait Wait... Don't Tell Me!' announcer Bill Kurtis talks about his journalism career
item.description: The announcer of "Wait Wait... Don't Tell Me!" --- the revered journalist Bill Kurtis -- has retired. His final show airs this weekend. NPR's A Martínez speaks with Kurtis about his career.
item.content:encoded: The announcer of "Wait Wait... Don't Tell Me!" --- the revered journalist Bill Kurtis -- has retired. His final show airs this weekend. NPR's A Martínez speaks with Kurtis about his career.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
